description: Remove PyArrow fallback from LiberoYoumuDataset, enforce Rust-only backend with fail-fast behavior, and verify e2e training works

userStories.title: Remove PyArrow fallback from LiberoYoumuDataset
userStories.description: As a developer, I want LiberoYoumuDataset to use only the Rust backend so the code is simpler and encoding issues are caught immediately rather than silently falling back to a slow path.
acceptanceCriteria: Import ParquetReaderCachePy, read_list_row_range_py, read_row_range_py unconditionally at module top level (no try/except ImportError)
Remove _HAS_RUST flag, _use_rust instance variable, and all if self._use_rust / else branches
Remove _pf_cache dict, _get_pf() method, and all PyArrow-based read paths in _read_list_column_range() and _read_image_range()
Remove column_name parameter from _read_list_column_range() since it was only needed for PyArrow
Remove _state_column, _action_column, _image_column instance variables (only needed for PyArrow column-name reads)
Constructor smoke test: if read_list_row_range_py raises, re-raise as RuntimeError with message about incompatible parquet encoding
Simplify __getstate__ / __setstate__: only handle Rust cache, no PyArrow branch
Update docstrings to reflect Rust-only operation
python -m py_compile submodules/youmu/python/youmu/libero_dataset.py passes
userStories.notes: Keep state_column, action_column, image_column constructor params — still needed for _find_physical_column_index. The smoke test error message should say: 'Rust parquet reader failed on {test_file}: {error}. Ensure parquet files are written with use_dictionary=False.'

userStories.title: Update tests for Rust-only backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all existing LIBERO tests to pass with the Rust-only LiberoYoumuDataset, confirming no regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: pytest tests/data/test_libero_dataset_episode_per_file.py -v — all tests pass (0 skipped)
HF_DATASETS_CACHE=/mnt/local/localcache00/hf_datasets_cache pytest tests/data/test_youmu_lerobot_parity.py -v — all 12 tests pass (0 skipped)
pytest tests/data/test_libero_task_descriptions.py -v — all tests pass (0 skipped)
pytest tests/data/test_libero_metadata_jsonl.py -v — all 8 tests pass
If any test relied on PyArrow fallback behavior, update it to work with Rust-only
python -m py_compile on all changed test files passes
userStories.notes: Root FS is 97% full so HF_DATASETS_CACHE must be set for lerobot parity tests. The synthetic fixture in conftest.py already writes plain-encoded parquet, so synthetic tests should work with Rust-only. Real data at libero_64KB was re-encoded without dict encoding.

userStories.title: Run e2e training for 5 steps and verify loss decreases
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run the full training pipeline for 5 steps and confirm the Rust-only dataset produces correct data that leads to decreasing loss.
acceptanceCriteria: Set max_steps: 5 in configs/multimodal/qwen3_vl/qwen3_vl_libero.yaml (or pass as override)
Run: bash train.sh tasks/omni/train_qwen_vl_libero.py configs/multimodal/qwen3_vl/qwen3_vl_libero.yaml
Training launches without import or config errors
All 5 steps complete without crash
Loss at step 1 is finite (not NaN/inf)
Loss at step 5 is strictly less than loss at step 1
No PyArrow fallback warnings in logs
Restore max_steps to original value after verification
userStories.notes: Training requires NPROC_PER_NODE>=2 for FSDP2 and VEOMNI_USE_LIGER_KERNEL=0 to avoid Triton bf16 errors. Previous run showed step 1 loss=4.0726, step 2 loss=2.3834. Config already points to libero_64KB and uses youmu backend.
